name: python3-unicorn
title: python3-unicorn
description:
Lightweight CPU emulator framework (Python 3 bindings)
Unicorn is a CPU emulator which is based on the CPU emulation code from
QEMU. Unlike QEMU, it focuses on the emulation of CPU code without a
full system emulation, but with the goal to make the emulation
extensible and easy to integrate in custom tools. Unicorn provides
language bindings for Python, Ruby, and Rust (among others) and has
instrumentation hooks for various CPU events. This makes Unicorn well
suited for malware analysis and reverse engineering.
.
This package installs the Python 3 bindings.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-unicorn because Lightweight CPU emulator framework (Python 3 bindings).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-unicorn as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-unicorn with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-unicorn --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
